$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; usesContext carried verbatim (termId + taxonomy). artisanpack/postPreview intentionally NOT added — term-description is an archive-context block, not a post-context block. supports carried verbatim.

Previews via createEntityPlaceholderEdit reading the stamped _resolvedTermDescription attribute, falling through to a labelled placeholder. Upstream edit.js queries the term entity through @wordpress/core-data, which the post editor doesn't populate (termId/taxonomy context is archive-only).
Dynamic block — returns null; markup produced server-side from stamped _resolvedTermDescription attribute.
Bidirectional core/term-description ↔ artisanpack/term-description block transforms for the rollout window.
Inline SVG inserter icon (editor lacks dashicons.css).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.

Single v1 entry ported to TypeScript: migrates the legacy top-level textAlign attribute into the canonical block-support shape via the vendored migrate-text-align helper. Save returns null — block is server-rendered.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx previews via createEntityPlaceholderEdit rather than upstream edit.js — the post editor doesn't populate termId/taxonomy block context, so upstream's entity-querying edit renders empty for any block placed outside a true archive context.
term-description is server-rendered: the front-end markup is produced by the Blade/React/Vue renderers from the stamped _resolvedTermDescription attribute, not by save().
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that do not exist upstream — required to coexist with core/term-description during the V1 rollout.
